q: What essential oil extracted from the hybrid mint Mentha × piperita is widely used as a flavoring?
e: Peppermint oil is the essential oil of the hybrid mint plant Mentha × piperita, obtained by steam distillation of fresh leaves. It is used to flavor candy, toothpaste, and liqueurs, and has antispasmodic medicinal uses.

q: What legume seed, available dried or fresh, is one of the most widely cultivated food plants in the world?
e: A bean is the seed of several plants in the legume family (Fabaceae). Beans are a dietary staple worldwide, consumed dried, canned, or fresh, and are a primary source of plant protein and fiber.

q: What coarse, purified wheat middling made from durum wheat is used to make pasta, couscous, and porridge?
e: Semolina is the coarse, purified wheat middlings of durum wheat, produced during the milling process. It is the primary ingredient in dried pasta, couscous, and many Middle Eastern and North African porridges.

q: Which rhizome from the species Alpinia galanga is used as a spice in Southeast Asian cooking and has a sharper, more peppery flavor than ginger?
e: Alpinia galanga (greater galangal) is a plant in the ginger family whose rhizome is used as a spice in Thai, Indonesian, and Malaysian cuisines. It has a sharper, more citrusy flavor than common ginger and is a key ingredient in dishes such as tom kha soup.
